tests.scenario: Show premium label on a locked course card
steps: Sign in as a non-premium user.
Open a screen that lists courses.
tests.expected_result: The locked course card shows that it belongs to the premium plan before the user starts checkout.

tests.scenario: Show plan status on a locked report feature
steps: Sign in as a non-premium user.
Open a screen that contains a locked report feature.
Observe the feature before tapping it.
tests.expected_result: The feature is identified as free or premium before the user attempts to open it.

tests.scenario: Display price and billing period before purchase confirmation
tests.expected_result: The summary screen shows the subscription price and billing period before the user confirms purchase.

tests.scenario: Display renewal and cancellation information before purchase confirmation
steps: Navigate to the purchase summary screen.
tests.expected_result: The summary screen shows the renewal condition and cancellation channel before the user confirms purchase.

tests.scenario: Save one reminder time for each check-in period
steps: Set reminder times for morning, afternoon, and evening.
tests.expected_result: The application stores and displays one saved reminder time for each of the three check-in periods.

tests.scenario: Edit existing reminder times
steps: Open reminder settings with existing saved times.
Change the reminder time for each period.
tests.expected_result: The application displays the updated reminder times for morning, afternoon, and evening.

tests.scenario: Submit a missed check-in within the allowed window
steps: Open the app within 12 hours after that period closes.
Select the missed period and submit answers.
tests.expected_result: The application accepts the missed check-in and stores it under the selected period.

tests.scenario: Block a missed check-in after the allowed window
steps: Wait more than 12 hours after that period closes.
Open the app and try to submit the missed period.
tests.expected_result: The application does not allow submission for that expired missed check-in period.

tests.scenario: Generate a report for a 14-day range with saved entries
steps: Save mood entries, symptom answers, and journal entries across 14 days.
tests.expected_result: The report contains mood trends, symptom trends, and references to journal entries from the selected range.

tests.scenario: Generate a report for a custom 30-day range
steps: Save entries across at least 30 days.
tests.expected_result: The report contains mood trends, symptom trends, and references to journal entries for the selected 30-day range.

tests.scenario: Export a generated report from the report screen
tests.expected_result: The application creates a downloadable file containing the report.

tests.scenario: Verify exported file contains the selected report range
steps: Generate a report for a known date range.
tests.expected_result: The downloaded file contains the report content for the selected date range.

tests.scenario: Restore saved data after sign-in on the same device
steps: Create an account with saved entries, reports, and settings.
Sign back in on the same device.
tests.expected_result: The saved entries, reports, and settings are visible after sign-in.

tests.scenario: Restore saved data after sign-in on a second device
steps: Create an account with saved entries, reports, and settings on device A.
Sign in to the same account on device B.
Open the account data on device B.
tests.expected_result: Device B shows the saved entries, reports, and settings from the account.

tests.scenario: Open privacy screen and review current settings
tests.expected_result: The privacy screen shows the current data-sharing permissions and privacy settings.

tests.scenario: Change a privacy setting from the privacy screen
steps: Change one data-sharing permission and save.
tests.expected_result: The updated privacy setting is saved and shown on the privacy screen.

tests.scenario: Create and reuse custom labels in later check-ins
steps: Create one custom emotion label and one custom activity label.
Save the labels and open a later check-in.
tests.expected_result: The saved custom labels are available for selection in the later check-in.

tests.scenario: Persist custom labels after app relaunch
steps: Create and save one custom emotion label and one custom activity label.
Reopen the app and start a new check-in.
tests.expected_result: The previously saved custom labels are still available in the new check-in.

tests.scenario: Submit I do not know for a binary question
steps: Select the I do not know response.
tests.expected_result: The application accepts and stores the I do not know response.

tests.scenario: Review a stored I do not know response
steps: Submit I do not know for a binary self-assessment question.
Open the saved responses for that check-in.
tests.expected_result: The saved response shows I do not know for that question.
